  1. Pre-Trip Planning

Accommodations

Hotels by City

logistics
hotels
planning
Published

June 4, 2026

Overview

Accommodations for 16 nights across Italy (Pisa, Florence, Naples, Puglia).

Booking Strategy: - Book 2-3 months ahead for best selection - Prioritize location over luxury - Check cancellation policies - Read recent reviews

Pisa (1 Night: June 4-5)

Options

If Overnighting in Pisa:

Location Priority: Near Pisa Centrale station (easy train to Florence next morning)

Mid-Range Options: - Hotel Bologna - Near station, classic Italian hotel - Royal Victoria - Along Arno river, historic property - Hotel Pisa Tower - Modern, near tower (but touristy area)

Budget Options: - Airbnb near station - Hotel di Stefano

Luxury: - Grand Hotel Duomo - Near Leaning Tower

Booking Considerations

  • Early morning departure: Choose hotel near station
  • See the tower: Stay near Piazza dei Miracoli (but pricier)
  • Authentic Pisa: Along Arno away from tower

Florence (4 Nights: June 5-9)

Location Priorities

Best Neighborhoods:

1. Santa Maria Novella (Near SMN Station) - Walking distance from train arrival - Central to major sites - Good restaurant options - Can be touristy

2. San Lorenzo / Mercato Centrale - Authentic neighborhood - Great food markets - Near Medici Chapels - Less touristy than Duomo area

3. Oltrarno (Across Arno) - Local character, artisan workshops - Excellent restaurants - Slightly farther from major museums - Quieter at night

4. Santa Croce - Vibrant piazza, good nightlife - Walking distance to all museums - Mix of local and tourist

Avoid: Immediate Duomo area (too touristy, noisy)

Hotel Options

Mid-Range (€100-200/night): - Hotel Davanzati - Near Duomo, family-run - Hotel Dali - San Lorenzo area, great value - Plus Florence - Modern, near SMN - Hotel Cestelli - Oltrarno, boutique

Luxury (€250+/night): - Hotel Lungarno - Arno views, Ferragamo family property - Portrait Firenze - Luxury boutique on Arno - Hotel Savoy - Piazza Repubblica, Rocco Forte

Budget (€60-100/night): - Airbnb in San Lorenzo or Santa Croce - Hotel Casci - Near San Marco - Hostel Archi Rossi (private rooms available)

Booking Considerations

  • Elevator: Many buildings are old, no elevator
  • AC: Essential for June heat
  • Noise: Request interior courtyard room if light sleeper
  • Breakfast: Often included, but Florence has great cafes

Naples (2 Nights: June 10-12)

Location Priorities

Best Areas:

1. Chiaia / Vomero - Upscale neighborhoods - Safer, quieter - Good restaurants - Metro/funicular access

2. Lungomare (Waterfront) - Sea views - Walking distance to Castel dell’Ovo - Beautiful promenade - More expensive

3. Centro Storico (Historic Center) - Walking distance to Spaccanapoli - Authentic Naples energy - Can be noisy and chaotic - Watch belongings

Avoid: Immediate Napoli Centrale station area (sketchy at night)

Hotel Options

Mid-Range (€80-150/night): - Hotel Piazza Bellini - Historic center, stylish - Decumani Hotel de Charme - Near MANN museum - Hotel San Francesco al Monte - Vomero, incredible views

Luxury (€200+/night): - Romeo Hotel - Modern luxury, waterfront - Grand Hotel Parker’s - Historic, views of bay and Vesuvius - Hotel Excelsior - On Via Partenope, classic

Budget (€50-80/night): - Airbnb in Chiaia or Vomero - Hotel Il Convento - Small, near Spaccanapoli - B&B Hotels Napoli - Chain, reliable, near port

Booking Considerations

  • Safety: Choose Chiaia, Vomero, or Lungomare
  • AC: Essential in June
  • Noise: Naples is loud - bring earplugs
  • Breakfast: Skip hotel breakfast, get pastries at local cafe

Puglia (6 Nights: June 13-19)

Status: Pending Tour Confirmation

Puglia accommodations will likely be included in the organized tour package (June 14-19).

Potential Scenarios

Scenario A: Tour Includes All Accommodations - Most likely - Hotels selected by tour operator - May rotate between towns or stay in one base - Typically 3-4 star properties

Scenario B: We Select Own Accommodations - Less likely if organized tour - Would coordinate with tour meeting points

Brindisi (1 Night: June 13)

Why: Arrive from Naples June 13, tour may start June 14

Options: - Hotel Orientale - Central, near port - Grande Albergo Internazionale - Historic - B&B near train station - Budget option

Possible Tour Locations

If tour rotates locations, potential overnight towns:

Alberobello: - Stay IN a trullo (unique experience) - Hotel options or trulli rentals

Lecce: - Centro storico (Baroque old town) - Near Piazza del Duomo

Polignano a Mare / Monopoli: - Coastal towns - Sea view hotels

Ostuni: - White city hilltop - Charming B&Bs

Masseria Stays

What: Traditional fortified farmhouses, now luxury hotels

Experience: - Rural Puglia setting - Often family-run - Excellent regional cuisine - Swimming pools - Olive groves and vineyards

Examples: - Masseria Torre Coccaro - Masseria San Domenico - Masseria Torre Maizza

Note: Premium masserie are €250-500/night, but tour may include mid-range options.

General Booking Tips

When to Book

  • 2-3 months ahead: Best selection and rates
  • Peak season (June-August): Book earlier
  • Flexible dates: Use booking.com to compare
  • Last minute: Risky in summer

What to Check

Before Booking: - [ ] Recent reviews (last 6 months) - [ ] Location on map (walkability) - [ ] Cancellation policy - [ ] Breakfast included? - [ ] Air conditioning (essential in June) - [ ] Elevator (if mobility concerns) - [ ] WiFi quality (check reviews) - [ ] Safe neighborhood (especially Naples)

Confirmation: - [ ] Save confirmation email - [ ] Screenshot booking details - [ ] Note hotel phone number - [ ] Add address to Google Maps - [ ] Check check-in time (usually 2-3 PM) - [ ] Note check-out time (usually 10-11 AM)

Booking Sites

Comparison: - Booking.com - Best selection, easy cancellation - Hotels.com - Loyalty rewards - Airbnb - Apartments, local character - Hotel direct websites - Sometimes better rates, perks

Tip: Check multiple sites, then book direct with hotel if same price (better service if issues arise).

Packing for Hotels

Bring: - [ ] Power adapters (Italy uses Type C, F, L plugs) - [ ] Small flashlight (power outages happen) - [ ] Earplugs (especially Naples, thin walls) - [ ] Portable phone charger - [ ] Travel-size toiletries (many hotels have minimal amenities)

Hotel Amenities Vary: - Hairdryer: Usually provided - Shampoo/soap: Often basic or not provided - Towels: Provided (but sometimes thin) - Slippers: Rarely provided - Robes: Only luxury hotels

Budget Planning

Estimated Costs (Per Night, Double Occupancy)

City Budget Mid-Range Luxury
Pisa €50-70 €80-120 €150+
Florence €60-100 €100-200 €250+
Naples €50-80 €80-150 €200+
Puglia TBD (tour) TBD (tour) €200+

Total Trip Estimate

Mid-Range Approach: - Pisa: 1 night × €100 = €100 - Florence: 4 nights × €150 = €600 - Naples: 2 nights × €120 = €240 - Puglia: 6 nights included in tour or ~€900 - Total: ~€1,840 (not including Puglia if tour-provided)

Accommodation Checklist
